rag_rules: Latched expressions are only valid in component implementations, not in type contracts or assume/guarantee clauses
Binary expressions in AGREE must have matching operand types; fix type mismatches by aligning operand types or correcting declarations
generic_rules: AGREE expressions must reference only visible identifiers or locally declared helpers; avoid package-qualified names not in scope
Assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes should relate visible inputs and outputs, not implementation-specific subcomponents
Use only AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations; avoid unresolved or invalid type references
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid component scopes
Guarantee expressions must have well-defined types; use type annotations to ensure correct operand matching
anti_patterns: Do not place latched expressions in component type contracts; they are only valid in implementations
Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid types in AGREE expressions; ensure all helpers and variables are properly declared and typed
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics related to latched expressions in wrong scopes
AGREE syntax for component type vs implementation annexes
